title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who joined a local community theatre group. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

content.title: All the World’s a Stage: Community Theatre

texts.D:
### Liam, The Leading Man
'I’m a shy person, so being cast as the lead was terrifying,' Liam admits. [47]Initially, the bright stage lights and the expectant silence of the audience were deeply disorienting.[/47] [53]I learned that acting is more about listening and reacting than just delivering lines.[/53] [51]The most difficult part was memorizing a fifty-page script while working a full-time job.[/51] [48]I was surprised to find that my public speaking anxiety at work completely vanished after the play ended.[/48] [50]I realized that my background in project management helped me coordinate the complex rehearsal schedules.[/50] It was a transformative experience that boosted my self-esteem.
texts.A:
### Sarah, The Set Designer
Sarah volunteered to build and paint the scenery. 'It was like a giant art project,' she says. [49]The experience taught me that the magic of theatre lies in the details that the audience might never even notice.[/49] [52]I hadn't anticipated the emotional bond that would form between the crew members during those late-night sessions.[/52] [47]I was quite startled by how quickly I adapted to the physical demands of moving heavy wooden flats.[/47] [54]What I valued most was the mentorship of a retired professional designer who showed me the ropes.[/54] [56]If I hadn't seen a 'volunteers needed' post on a local arts forum, I never would have joined.[/56] It was a creative awakening.
texts.B:
### Victor, The Costume Assistant
Victor helped sew and fit the period costumes. 'It’s a meticulous craft,' he notes. [51]I struggled with the high-pressure environment of quick changes during the actual performances.[/51] [48]I noticed that my general manual dexterity improved, which was a side effect I hadn't expected.[/48] [53]I realized that every costume tells a story about the character's status and state of mind.[/53] [50]I found that the precision I used in my previous career as a surgeon was perfect for the delicate needlework.[/50] [55]It took a while to adjust to the flamboyant personalities in the group, which felt overwhelming at first.[/55] [52]I’d initially worried it would be too frivolous, but the dedication was intense.
texts.C:
### Elena, The Stage Manager
'I’m the person with the clipboard and the headset,' Elena laughs. [47]I hadn't expected the mental fatigue of having to anticipate every possible technical failure during a show.[/47] [56]I wouldn't have found the group if my daughter hadn't encouraged me to find a hobby after I retired.[/56] [54]I was moved by the diverse backgrounds of the actors, all united by a passion for storytelling.[/54] [49]It changed how I view live performances; I’m much more aware of the invisible work behind the scenes now.[/49] [55]I found the initial chaos of the first technical rehearsal incredibly frustrating and stressful.[/55] [52]I’d been concerned that I wouldn't fit in, but the theatre family is incredibly inclusive.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by the physical or mental impact of the theatre environment?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) felt disoriented by lights/silence, Sarah (B) was startled by physical demands, and Elena (D) mentions mental fatigue.

questions.question: notes a positive personal improvement that transferred to another area of their life?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) lost his work-related speaking anxiety, and Victor (C) improved his manual dexterity.

questions.question: expresses a shift in their appreciation for the technical or invisible aspects of a craft?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) learned magic lies in unseen details, and Elena (D) is now aware of the invisible work behind scenes.

questions.question: mentions how their professional background was beneficial to the production?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) used project management for schedules, and Victor (C) used surgical precision for needlework.

questions.question: describes a specific logistical or memorization challenge that was difficult to manage?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) struggled with the 50-page script, and Victor (C) with the pressure of quick changes.

questions.question: admits to having had prior doubts or having formed an unexpected emotional connection?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) formed an unexpected bond with the crew, and Victor (C) worried it would be too frivolous.

questions.question: reflects on a deeper realization about the nature of acting or characterization?
questions.explanation: Liam (A) learned acting is about listening, and Victor (C) realized costumes tell a story.

questions.question: highlights the value of the guidance or the diversity of the people involved?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) valued the mentorship of the professional, and Elena (D) was moved by the diverse backgrounds.

questions.question: describes a period of frustration or adjustment to the group's dynamics or rehearsals?
questions.explanation: Victor (C) adjusted to flamboyant personalities, and Elena (D) found the first tech rehearsal chaotic.

questions.question: acknowledges the specific external factor that led to their involvement?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) saw a forum post, and Elena (D) was encouraged by her daughter.
